findings.label: RGV hotel developer / hospitality real estate
findings.notes: McAllen/Edinburg-area Sunil Wadhwani is a hospitality entrepreneur who has built hotels (La Quinta, Motel 6, Marriott) across Hidalgo County. Hospitality development is a real estate adjacent activity. Note: he was convicted in 2019 of bribing a Weslaco city commissioner over a Motel 6 hotel project — flagged as sensitive.

evidence.evidence_text: KVEO/ValleyCentral reporting identifies Sunil Wadhwani, 61, of McAllen as a hotel-owning businessman in Hidalgo County who bribed a Weslaco commissioner over a Motel 6 hotel deal
evidence.snippet: Sunil Wadhwani, 61, of McAllen — a wealthy businessman who owns hotels in Hidalgo County — paid at least $4,000 to Weslaco City Commissioner Gerardo "Jerry" Tafolla. In exchange, Tafolla voted to approve $300,000 in economic incentives for a hotel Wadhwani planned to build.
evidence.search_query: "Sunil Wadhwani" McAllen bribed commissioner hotel developer

findings.label: President of Castle Hospitality; hotel & McAllen City Center developer; Texas Regional Bank director
findings.notes: Michael Fallek runs Castle Hospitality, develops hotels and the McAllen City Center mixed-use project (10th & Expressway 83) and McAllen South Industrial Park (125 acres, ~1.8M sf). He's a Texas Regional Bank director and former McAllen P&Z chair. He's also been described as having 'helped launch and grow several successful McAllen-based businesses, primarily in hospitality and real estate.' He has since announced for McAllen District 5 (Haddad's vacated seat).

evidence.evidence_text: Texas Regional Bank director bio describes Fallek's Castle Hospitality role and major McAllen real estate developments
evidence.snippet: In 1998 he joined Castle Hospitality, LTD. – RGV Properties, Inc. – Boulevard Development Company, L.C., and became active in the ownership, operation, and development of hotels throughout Texas.
evidence.rule: named executive at real estate development firm

evidence.evidence_text: Fallek's own campaign site explicitly identifies his work as hospitality and real estate
evidence.snippet: Over the past 20 years, he has helped launch and grow several successful McAllen-based businesses, primarily in hospitality and real estate, contributing to local investment and the creation of thousands of jobs throughout the Rio Grande Valley.
evidence.search_query: "Michael Fallek" McAllen Texas Temple Emanuel AIPAC Israel
